task_description: Customer 'C-404' submitted a request affecting subscription 'sub_112'. First inspect the customer record and that subscription record, then review the customer's remaining subscriptions. The returned customer and subscription data contain the halt-request note and billing guidance relevant to whether only one subscription or all active subscriptions should be paused or cancelled. In this ticket, if the customer record shows that additional active subscriptions should also have upcoming billing halted, you must also pause or cancel every other active subscription on the account before you finish. Then verify the updated status of each affected subscription.

C-404.name: Alex Mercer
C-404.billing_guidance_note: If additional active subscriptions remain, upcoming billing cycles should also be halted.

sub_112.customer_request_note: The customer says they are unsure when they will use the service next and wants the primary subscription halted.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: The agent's lethargy manifests as a preference to defer permanent decisions, choosing pausing over full cancellation while still following the customer's billing guidance.
Even while choosing the less final pause route, the agent still has to inspect whether the customer note requires every active subscription on the account to be halted.
The low-energy route still needs to halt every remaining active subscription once the billing guidance says to do so.
expected_actions: Call pause_subscription on sub_889 if it is still active.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Being emotionally distant and reserved, the agent prefers a clean, permanent break (cancellation) over an accommodating temporary pause.
Unaffected by lethargy, the detached agent mechanically follows the instruction to audit the account without feeling burdened by the extra steps.
The agent applies the same distant, definitive resolution to the secondary subscription, severing all ties without hesitation.
